task_completion.description: Import the specified audio file into Ardour.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ardour timeline shows an imported audio region/waveform, or the import dialog is visible with '/home/ga/Audio/import_me.wav' selected.

task_completion.description: Split the audio clip at the 15-second mark.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The audio region on the timeline is split into two distinct clips, with the cut located at or very close to the 15.0-second mark on the time ruler.

task_completion.description: Move the second half of the audio to the beginning of the timeline.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An audio region (the segment originally from 15-30s) is positioned so its start aligns exactly with the 0.0s mark on the timeline.

task_completion.description: Move the first half of the audio to start at 14.0 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An audio region (the segment originally from 0-15s) is positioned so its start aligns with the 14.0s mark on the timeline, creating a 1-second overlap with the other region.
The track header on the left side of the Ardour timeline displays the name 'Ambient Loop' (or contains the keywords 'ambient' and 'loop').
The 'File -> Save' menu action is clicked, or the Ardour window title bar shows the session name without an unsaved indicator (like an asterisk).

integrity.description: The agent used the Ardour GUI to perform the audio editing tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Ardour application interface being actively used (timeline edits, track renaming), rather than just terminal windows.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by directly editing the Ardour session XML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal or text editors (like nano/vim) do not show the agent manually modifying the `.ardour` XML file to set region positions or track names.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual timeline and regions.
integrity.visual_evidence: Cursor movements, drag-and-drop actions, or split tool selections are visible on the Ardour timeline.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source file is '/home/ga/Audio/import_me.wav'. The track name must contain 'ambient' or 'loop' (case-insensitive). The split point is at 15.0 seconds. The back half (originally 15-30s) must be placed at 0.0s. The front half (originally 0-15s) must be placed at 14.0s. A tolerance of 0.5 seconds is allowed for all timeline positions.
